sourceMovie.title: Star Trek II: The Wrath of Khan
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Star Trek II: The Wrath of Khan works as a action movie with a point of view—fans quote moments, but they also argue about what the film is actually saying about power, desire, or survival. The action and adventure blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Guardians of the Galaxy
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Star Trek II: The Wrath of Khan clicked for you, Guardians of the Galaxy (2014) is a strong next stop: both trade in action and adventure, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: Star Wars: The Rise of Skywalker
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Star Trek II: The Wrath of Khan clicked for you, Star Wars: The Rise of Skywalker (2019) is a strong next stop: both trade in action and adventure, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

If Star Trek II: The Wrath of Khan clicked for you, 2010 (1984) is a strong next stop: both trade in science fiction and thriller, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Star Trek II: The Wrath of Khan?
faq.answer: Try Guardians of the Galaxy, Star Wars: The Rise of Skywalker, Ender's Game—each shares DNA with Star Trek II: The Wrath of Khan while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Star Trek II: The Wrath of Khan?
faq.answer: Star Trek II: The Wrath of Khan (1982) is best described as Action, Adventure, Science Fiction, Thriller. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Star Trek II: The Wrath of Khan part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "Star Trek: The Original Series Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
